name: Pumpkin Seed Extract
historicalUse: Pumpkin seeds have been used for urinary and parasitic conditions across Native American, Central American, Arabic, Ottoman, and Eastern European medical traditions for centuries. They appear in Ottoman court dietary medicine specifically for male urinary health. Indigenous peoples of the Americas used them for bladder and kidney conditions long before European contact. They entered European pharmacopoeias by the 16th century.
modernEvidence: Pumpkin seed oil and extract provide delta-7-sterols (cucurbitacins), zinc, selenium, and a unique fatty acid profile. Clinical studies demonstrate benefits in BPH symptoms, overactive bladder in both men and women, and nocturia reduction. Unlike most other urinary support botanicals, pumpkin seed extract has evidence for both male prostate support and female bladder urgency/overactive bladder.
mechanisms: Delta-7-sterol activity — modulates DHT receptors in prostate and bladder tissue
Bladder smooth muscle tone — reduces detrusor overactivity and urgency
Zinc provision — supports prostate epithelial zinc-dependent enzymes
Antioxidant — tocopherols and selenium reduce prostate oxidative stress
benefits: Supports healthy urinary flow in men and women
Promotes normal bladder tone and continence
Contributes to antioxidant defense in urinary tract tissues
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. GRAS status for pumpkin seed. No significant adverse effects in clinical trials.
contraindications: No known absolute contraindications at supplement doses
drugInteractions: No clinically significant drug interactions identified at standard doses
physicianNotes: Notably one of the few urinary support botanicals with evidence in women for overactive bladder and urgency — not just BPH support. Appropriate in mixed-gender clinical practice. Well tolerated and appropriate for long-term use.
patientGuidance: Pumpkin seed extract is appropriate for both men and women seeking urinary comfort support. Take with meals for best tolerability.
